mn25:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn25:10.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the third contemplatives and brahmins thought:
mn25:10.10: There they ate greens … feeding on fallen fruit.
mn25:10.11: But in the last month of the hot season, when grass and water ran short, their bodies became emaciated to an extreme degree.
mn25:10.12: When their bodies had become emaciated to an extreme degree, their *bala* and energy [*balavīriyaṁ*] declined; when their *bala* and energy had declined, liberation of mind [*cetovimutti*] declined; when liberation of mind [*cetovimutti*] had declined, they returned to that very bait laid down by Māra and to those worldly lures.
mn25:10.13: There they came right up to it, infatuated, and ate the food.
mn25:10.14: While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:10.15: Thus those second contemplatives and brahmins also did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:10.16: What if we were to establish a base relying on that bait laid down by Māra and on those worldly lures? Having established a base there, we would eat the food from that bait laid down by Māra and from those worldly lures without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, we would not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, we would not become heedless; being unheedful, we would not be at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.’
mn25:10.17: They established a base relying on that bait laid down by Māra and on those worldly lures.
mn25:10.18: Having established a base there, they ate the food from that bait laid down by Māra and from those worldly lures without coming right up to it, without infatuation.
mn25:10.19: While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, they did not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, they did not become heedless; being unheedful, they were not at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:10.2: ‘Those first contemplatives and brahmins came right up to that bait laid down by Māra and to those worldly lures …
mn25:10.20: But they still held such views as these—
mn25:10.21: ‘The world is eternal,’ or ‘The world is not eternal’;
mn25:10.22: ‘The world is finite,’ or ‘The world is infinite’;
mn25:10.23: ‘The living being is the same as the body,’ or ‘The living being is one thing and the body another’;
mn25:10.24: ‘The Tathāgata exists after death,’ or ‘The Tathāgata does not exist after death,’ or ‘The Tathāgata both exists and does not exist after death,’ or ‘The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death.’
mn25:10.25: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the third contemplatives and brahmins also did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:10.26: Just as, *bhikkhus*, were those third deer, so I say these third contemplatives and brahmins are comparable to them.
mn25:10.3: Thus those first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:10.4: And those second contemplatives and brahmins thought like this:
mn25:10.5: “Those first contemplatives and brahmins came right up to that bait laid down by Māra and to those worldly lures …
mn25:10.6: Thus those first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:10.7: What if we were to refrain entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?”
mn25:10.8: They refrained entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures.
mn25:10.9: Keeping away from what involves fear, they dwelt having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*].
mn25:11.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the fourth contemplatives and brahmins thought:
mn25:11.10: And those third contemplatives and brahmins thought like this: Those first contemplatives and brahmins …
mn25:11.11: Thus those first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:11.12: And those second contemplatives and brahmins thought like this: Those first contemplatives and brahmins …
mn25:11.13: Thus those first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:11.14: What if we were to refrain entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?’
mn25:11.15: They refrained entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures …
mn25:11.16: Thus those second contemplatives and brahmins also did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:11.17: What if we were to establish a base relying on that bait laid down by Māra and on those worldly lures?
mn25:11.18: Having established a base there, we would eat the food from that bait laid down by Māra and from those worldly lures without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, we would not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, we would not become heedless; being unheedful, we would not be at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:11.19: They established a base relying on that bait laid down by Māra and on those worldly lures.
mn25:11.2: ‘Those first contemplatives and brahmins came right up to that bait laid down by Māra …
mn25:11.20: Having established a base there, they ate the food from that bait laid down by Māra and from those worldly lures without coming right up to it, without infatuation.
mn25:11.21: While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, they did not become intoxicated.
mn25:11.22: Not being intoxicated, they did not become heedless.
mn25:11.23: Being unheedful, they were not at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:11.24: But they still held such views as these: ‘The world is eternal’ …
mn25:11.25: ‘The Tathāgata neither exists nor does not exist after death.’
mn25:11.26: Thus those third contemplatives and brahmins also did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:11.27: What if we were to establish a base where there is no access for Māra and his retinue?
mn25:11.28: Having established a base there, we would eat the food from that bait laid down by Māra and from those worldly lures without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, we would not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, we would not become heedless; being unheedful, we would not be at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:11.29: They established a base where there was no access for Māra and his retinue.
mn25:11.3: Thus those first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:11.30: Having established a base there, they ate the food from that bait laid down by Māra and from those worldly lures without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, they did not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, they did not become heedless; being unheedful, they were not at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:11.31: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the fourth contemplatives and brahmins escaped Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:11.32: Just as, *bhikkhus*, were those fourth deer, so I say these fourth contemplatives and brahmins are comparable to them.
mn25:11.4: And those second contemplatives and brahmins thought like this:
mn25:11.6: Thus those first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:11.7: What if we were to refrain entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?’
mn25:11.8: They refrained entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures …
mn25:11.9: Thus those second contemplatives and brahmins also did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:12.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the region inaccessible to Māra and his retinue?
mn25:12.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome *dhammas*, a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, with thought [*savitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
mn25:12.3: This is called, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* who has blinded Māra, who, having destroyed the track, has gone beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:13.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the settling of thought and examination [*vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā*], with inner clarity and unification of mind [*cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, without thought [*avitakkaṁ*] and without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijaṁ*].
mn25:13.2: This is called, *bhikkhus* … beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:14.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the fading away of *pīti*, a *bhikkhu* dwells equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati* and fully aware [*sampajāno*], and experiences *sukha* with the body. He enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*, of which the noble ones declare: ‘Equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*satimā*], one who dwells in *sukha*.’
mn25:14.2: This is called, *bhikkhus* … beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:15.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the giving up of *sukha* and the giving up of *dukkha*, and with the earlier disappearance of joy and distress, a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*, which is neither-painful-nor-pleasant, with purity of equanimity [*upekkhā*] and *sati* [*upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ*].
mn25:15.2: This is called, *bhikkhus* … beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:16.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the complete transcending of *saññā*s of form, with the disappearance of *saññā*s of resistance, not attending to *saññā*s of diversity, thinking, ‘Space is boundless,’ a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn25:16.2: This is called, *bhikkhus* … beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:17.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the complete transcending of the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘Consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is boundless,’ a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn25:17.2: This is called, *bhikkhus* … beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:18.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the complete transcending of the dimension of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘There is nothing,’ a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn25:18.2: This is called, *bhikkhus* … beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:19.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the complete transcending of the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the dimension of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn25:19.2: This is called, *bhikkhus* … beyond the sight of the Evil One.
mn25:2.1: “*Bhikkhus*, it is not for this reason that the bait-layer lays down bait for the deer:
mn25:2.2: ‘Let the deer eat this bait I have laid down, and so live long, be beautiful, and endure for a long time.’
mn25:2.3: Rather, *bhikkhus*, it is for this reason that the bait-layer lays down bait for the deer:
mn25:2.4: ‘When the deer come right up to this bait I have laid down and, infatuated, eat the food, then, while eating the food after coming right up to it, infatuated, they will become intoxicated; being intoxicated, they will become heedless; being heedless, they will be at my mercy in this baited place.’
mn25:20.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, with the complete transcending of the dimension of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the cessation of *saññā* and *vedanā* [*saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ*]. And seeing with discernment [*paññāya*], his *āsavas* are exhausted.
mn25:20.2: This is called, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* who has blinded Māra, who, having destroyed the track, has gone beyond the sight of the Evil One, who has crossed over attachment in the world.”
mn25:20.4: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* delighted in the *Bhagavā*’s words.
mn25:20.5: The Bait is finished, the fifth.
mn25:3.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the first herd of deer came right up to that bait laid down by the bait-layer and, infatuated, ate the food. While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.
mn25:3.2: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the first herd of deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:4.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the second herd of deer thought:
mn25:4.10: There they came right up to it, infatuated, and ate the food.
mn25:4.11: While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.
mn25:4.12: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the second herd of deer also did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:4.2: ‘Those first deer came right up to that bait laid down by the bait-layer and, infatuated, ate the food.
mn25:4.3: While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.
mn25:4.4: Thus those first deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:4.5: What if we were to refrain entirely from eating the bait, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?’
mn25:4.6: They refrained entirely from eating the bait, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwelt having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*].
mn25:4.7: But in the last month of the hot season, when grass and water ran short, their bodies became emaciated to an extreme degree.
mn25:4.8: When their bodies had become emaciated to an extreme degree, their *bala* and energy [*balavīriyaṁ*] declined.
mn25:4.9: And when their *bala* and energy had declined, they returned to that very bait laid down by the bait-layer.
mn25:5.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the third herd of deer thought:
mn25:5.10: When their bodies had become emaciated to an extreme degree, their *bala* and energy [*balavīriyaṁ*] declined.
mn25:5.11: And when their *bala* and energy had declined, they returned to that very bait laid down by the bait-layer.
mn25:5.12: There they came right up to it, infatuated, and ate the food.
mn25:5.13: While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.
mn25:5.14: Thus those second deer also did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:5.15: What if we were to establish a base relying on that bait laid down by the bait-layer?
mn25:5.16: Having established a base there, we would eat the food from that bait laid down by the bait-layer without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, we would not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, we would not become heedless; being unheedful, we would not be at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.’
mn25:5.17: They established a base relying on that bait laid down by the bait-layer.
mn25:5.18: Having established a base there, they ate the food from that bait laid down by the bait-layer without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, they did not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, they did not become heedless; being unheedful, they were not at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.
mn25:5.19: Then, *bhikkhus*, it occurred to the bait-layer and his attendants:
mn25:5.2: ‘Those first deer came right up to that bait laid down by the bait-layer …
mn25:5.20: ‘These third deer must be sly and crafty; they must be powerful creatures from another stock.
mn25:5.21: For they consume this bait that has been laid down, yet we do not know either their coming or their going.
mn25:5.22: What if we were to surround this baited place all around, section by section, with great barricades of stakes? Then perhaps we might see the base of the third herd of deer, where they might be caught.’
mn25:5.23: So they surrounded that baited place all around, section by section, with great barricades of stakes.
mn25:5.24: And indeed, *bhikkhus*, the bait-layer and his attendants saw the base of the third herd of deer, where they were caught.
mn25:5.25: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the third herd of deer also did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:5.3: thus those first deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:5.4: And those second deer thought like this:
mn25:5.5: “Those first deer came right up to that bait laid down by the bait-layer …
mn25:5.6: thus those first deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:5.7: What if we were to refrain entirely from eating the bait, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?”
mn25:5.8: They refrained entirely from eating the bait, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwelt having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*].
mn25:5.9: But in the last month of the hot season, when grass and water ran short, their bodies became emaciated to an extreme degree.
mn25:6.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the fourth herd of deer thought:
mn25:6.10: And those third deer thought like this:
mn25:6.12: thus those first deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:6.13: And those second deer thought like this:
mn25:6.15: thus those first deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:6.16: What if we were to refrain entirely from eating the bait, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?’
mn25:6.17: They refrained entirely from eating the bait …
mn25:6.18: thus those second deer also did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:6.19: What if we were to establish a base relying on that bait laid down by the bait-layer? Having established a base there, we would eat the food from that bait laid down by the bait-layer without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, we would not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, we would not become heedless; being unheedful, we would not be at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.’
mn25:6.20: They established a base relying on that bait laid down by the bait-layer. Having established a base there, they ate the food from that bait laid down by the bait-layer without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, they did not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, they did not become heedless; being unheedful, they were not at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.
mn25:6.21: Then it occurred to the bait-layer and his attendants:
mn25:6.22: “These third deer must be sly and crafty, powerful creatures from another stock, and yet they consume this bait that has been laid down.
mn25:6.23: But we do not know either their coming or their going.
mn25:6.24: What if we were to surround this baited place all around, section by section, with great barricades of stakes? Then perhaps we might see the base of the third herd of deer, where they might be caught.”
mn25:6.25: So they surrounded that baited place all around, section by section, with great barricades of stakes.
mn25:6.26: And the bait-layer and his attendants saw the base of the third herd of deer, where they were caught.
mn25:6.27: Thus those third deer also did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:6.28: What if we were to establish a base where there is no access for the bait-layer and his attendants? Having established a base there, we would eat the food from that bait laid down by the bait-layer without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, we would not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, we would not become heedless; being unheedful, we would not be at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.’
mn25:6.29: They established a base where there was no access for the bait-layer and his attendants.
mn25:6.3: thus those first deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:6.30: Having established a base there, they ate the food from that bait laid down by the bait-layer without coming right up to it, without infatuation. While eating the food there without coming right up to it, without infatuation, they did not become intoxicated; not being intoxicated, they did not become heedless; being unheedful, they were not at the bait-layer’s mercy in that baited place.
mn25:6.31: Then, *bhikkhus*, it occurred to the bait-layer and his attendants:
mn25:6.32: ‘These fourth deer must be sly and crafty, powerful creatures from another stock.
mn25:6.33: For they consume this bait that has been laid down, yet we do not know either their coming or their going.
mn25:6.34: What if we were to surround this baited place all around, section by section, with great barricades of stakes? Then perhaps we might see the base of the fourth herd of deer, where they might be caught.’
mn25:6.35: So they surrounded that baited place all around, section by section, with great barricades of stakes.
mn25:6.36: But, *bhikkhus*, neither the bait-layer nor his attendants saw the base of the fourth herd of deer, where they might be caught.
mn25:6.37: Then, *bhikkhus*, it occurred to the bait-layer and his attendants:
mn25:6.38: ‘If we disturb the fourth herd of deer, then when they are disturbed, others will be disturbed; when those are disturbed, still others will be disturbed.
mn25:6.39: Thus all the deer will completely escape from this baited place.
mn25:6.4: And those second deer thought like this:
mn25:6.40: What if we were simply to leave the fourth herd of deer alone?’
mn25:6.41: And so, *bhikkhus*, the bait-layer and his attendants left the fourth herd of deer alone.
mn25:6.42: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the fourth herd of deer escaped the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:6.6: thus those first deer did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:6.7: What if we were to refrain entirely from eating the bait, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?”
mn25:6.8: They refrained entirely from eating the bait …
mn25:6.9: thus those second deer also did not escape the bait-layer’s power and sway.
mn25:7.1: *Bhikkhus*, I have made this simile for the purpose of conveying the meaning.
mn25:7.2: And this is the meaning here—
mn25:7.3: ‘bait’ is a designation for these five strands of sensual pleasure.
mn25:7.4: ‘the bait-layer’ is a designation for Māra the Evil One.
mn25:7.5: ‘the bait-layer’s attendants’ is a designation for Māra’s retinue.
mn25:7.6: ‘the herds of deer’ is a designation for contemplatives and brahmins.
mn25:8.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the first contemplatives and brahmins came right up to that bait laid down by Māra and to those worldly lures, and, infatuated, ate the food.
mn25:8.2: While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:8.3: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:8.4: Just as, *bhikkhus*, were those first deer, so I say these first contemplatives and brahmins are comparable to them.
mn25:9.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the second contemplatives and brahmins thought:
mn25:9.10: When their bodies had become emaciated to an extreme degree, their *bala* and energy [*balavīriyaṁ*] declined.
mn25:9.11: When their *bala* and energy had declined, liberation of mind [*cetovimutti*] declined.
mn25:9.12: When liberation of mind [*cetovimutti*] had declined, they returned to that very bait laid down by Māra and to those worldly lures.
mn25:9.13: There they came right up to it, infatuated, and ate the food.
mn25:9.14: While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:9.15: Thus, *bhikkhus*, the second contemplatives and brahmins also did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:9.16: Just as, *bhikkhus*, were those second deer, so I say these second contemplatives and brahmins are comparable to them.
mn25:9.2: ‘Those first contemplatives and brahmins came right up to that bait laid down by Māra and to those worldly lures, and, infatuated, ate the food.
mn25:9.3: While eating the food there after coming right up to it, infatuated, they became intoxicated; being intoxicated, they became heedless; being heedless, they were at Māra’s mercy in that bait and in those worldly lures.
mn25:9.4: Thus those first contemplatives and brahmins did not escape Māra’s power and sway.
mn25:9.5: What if we were to refrain entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwell having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*]?’
mn25:9.6: They refrained entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwelt having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*].
mn25:9.7: They refrained entirely from the bait-food and worldly lures, and, keeping away from what involves fear, dwelt having plunged into forest regions [*araññāyatanāni*].
mn25:9.8: There they ate greens, millet, wild rice, moss, husks, grain fragments, rinsings, oil-cake, grass, and cow dung. They kept themselves alive on roots and fruits of the forest, feeding on fallen fruit.
mn25:9.9: But in the last month of the hot season, when grass and water ran short, their bodies became emaciated to an extreme degree.
